prompt: Inside the Polymarket 'Eurovision Winner 2026' event (50 countries), take the top 10 countries by current implied probability and chart total whale-position value against implied probability per country — does whale conviction match the headline odds heading into the May 16 final?
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
Whale conviction matches the headline odds with near-perfect alignment heading into the May 16 final. As of May 2, 2026, the correlation between implied probability and total whale-position value for the top 10 countries is **0.999**, indicating that large-scale bettors are positioned in strict lockstep with market prices.

### Eurovision Winner 2026: Top 10 Countries by Implied Probability

| Country | Implied Probability | Total Whale-Position Value | Whale Count | Conviction Level |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Finland | 34.6% | $413,933.57 | 23 | Extreme |
| Greece | 17.8% | $204,946.86 | 19 | Extreme |
| Denmark | 12.8% | $139,484.55 | 17 | Extreme |
| Australia | 7.6% | $81,998.32 | 16 | Extreme |
| France | 6.8% | $73,615.52 | 12 | Extreme |
| Israel | 4.0% | $45,120.74 | 7 | High |
| Italy | 2.8% | $29,612.43 | 5 | High |
| Sweden | 2.6% | $28,281.53 | 4 | High |
| Romania | 2.6% | $27,307.78 | 5 | High |
| Ukraine | 1.4% | $15,472.10 | 2 | High |

The scatter chart shows a linear relationship where whale interest intensifies significantly as winning odds increase. **Finland** is the dominant "smart money" favorite, commanding over $413,000 in whale positions—more...
